skill.name: fundamentals-gate
skill.description: Review code fundamentals such as naming, function focus, reuse, structure, comments, and simple maintainability standards.
skill.summary: Run a non-blocking fundamentals review for readable, maintainable code.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found these are Markdown code spans, examples, or ordinary prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, data exfiltration, prompt injection text, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown backticks, fenced examples, inline code identifiers, and template text. They do not execute shell commands or invoke Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown and the flagged text is inside quoted questions, checklists, fenced examples, or inline code spans. There is no Ruby source file, shell invocation, script block, or command execution context.

low_findings.title: Static Cryptography and Reconnaissance Flags Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary words, examples, or Markdown syntax. No hashing algorithm use, encryption code, host inspection, or environment probing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain prose about code quality or naming examples, not cryptographic API calls or reconnaissance commands. The surrounding context confirms an instructional checklist with no executable payload.

content.value_statement: Code that works can still be hard to maintain. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a practical review of naming, structure, reuse, comments, and function focus.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether variable and function names are clear and intention revealing.
Reviews functions for single responsibility, size, parameters, and hidden side effects.
Identifies duplication that may deserve extraction without forcing premature abstraction.
Evaluates structure, separation of concerns, formatting consistency, and related code grouping.
Reviews comments for useful intent and flags obvious or commented-out code.
Produces pass or suggestion-style review responses rather than hard blockers.
limitations: It provides qualitative review guidance, not automated static analysis results.
It does not execute tests, parse full repositories, or calculate quality metrics by itself.
It treats issues as suggestions and does not decide release readiness alone.
It relies on the model receiving enough code context to make fair judgments.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Review changed code for readability issues before requesting human review.

use_cases.title: Coach Junior Engineers
use_cases.description: Turn maintainability feedback into clear, Socratic questions and non-blocking suggestions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Reviews
use_cases.description: Apply a shared checklist for names, functions, reuse, structure, and comments across code reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Fundamentals Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fundamentals gate to review this code. Focus on naming, function focus, reuse, structure, and comments. Return PASS or SUGGESTIONS.

prompt_templates.title: Suggestion-Only Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request with the fundamentals gate. Treat findings as suggestions only. Explain each suggestion with a short reason and a practical improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Mentor-Style Questions
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the fundamentals gate and phrase the most important feedback as Socratic questions. Prioritize learning value over direct rewrites.

prompt_templates.title: Repository Pattern Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the fundamentals gate across these files. Identify repeated naming, structure, duplication, function-size, and comment patterns. Separate team-level patterns from local suggestions.

output_examples.input: A small service method with clear names, focused logic, and no obvious duplication.
output_examples.output: FUNDAMENTALS GATE: PASSED. Naming is clear, functions are focused, and the structure is easy to follow. No fundamentals suggestions are needed.

output_examples.input: A function with abbreviated variables, multiple responsibilities, and repeated calculation logic.
output_examples.output: FUNDAMENTALS GATE: SUGGESTIONS. Consider renaming abbreviated variables, splitting the function by responsibility, and extracting the repeated calculation after confirming the pattern repeats.

output_examples.input: A prototype feature with rough names but clear time pressure and limited user impact.
output_examples.output: FUNDAMENTALS GATE: SUGGESTIONS. The code can proceed, but capture follow-up items for clearer names and smaller helper functions when the prototype stabilizes.

best_practices: Provide the changed code and nearby context so the review can judge intent fairly.
Ask for suggestions separately from blockers to keep maintainability feedback proportional.
Use team conventions as the baseline when they differ from generic style advice.
anti_patterns: Using the gate as a strict release blocker for minor readability concerns.
Requesting broad refactors without showing enough code context or team standards.
Applying every suggestion mechanically when the existing codebase has a deliberate pattern.

faq.question: Does this skill block code from shipping?
faq.answer: No. The skill treats fundamentals issues as suggestions, not blockers.

faq.question: What does the skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews naming, function focus, reuse, structure, comments, and basic maintainability patterns.

faq.question: Can it replace automated linters?
faq.answer: No. It complements linters by reviewing readability and design judgment that tools often miss.

faq.question: Can it review large repositories?
faq.answer: It can guide multi-file reviews when given relevant files, but it does not index a repository by itself.

faq.question: Is the feedback suitable for junior developers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes question-based feedback that supports learning without harsh gatekeeping.

faq.question: Does it enforce one naming style?
faq.answer: No. It favors clear names and consistent local patterns over rigid universal rules.
